title: Architectural Material Lab & Ruin Exhibition
description: The user curates a virtual architecture exhibition featuring a damaged ancient helmet artifact displayed on pedestals made from ShaderBall forms. They configure lighting rigs, adjust material properties, position display pedestals along a gallery floor, and control an orbital camera to compose and capture the perfect architectural showcase scene.
prompt:
Create a single HTML file using Three.js r170 (via importmap from cdn.jsdelivr.net) that implements an interactive architectural exhibition gallery. Load DamagedHelmet.glb as the centerpiece artifact and load ShaderBall.glb three times as display pedestals arranged in a triangular formation on a reflective gallery floor. Setup: Create a 20x20 ground plane with MeshStandardMaterial (metalness:0.3, roughness:0.2, color:#2a2a2a) to simulate polished concrete. Place the DamagedHelmet at center elevated on the middle ShaderBall pedestal (scaled to 2x). Place two flanking ShaderBall pedestals at positions (-3,0,-1) and (3,0,-1) scaled to 1.5x. Add a subtle grid texture to the floor. Use OrbitControls with damping enabled. Lighting: Add one warm DirectionalLight (intensity:2, color:#ffe0b2) and one cool DirectionalLight (intensity:1.2, color:#b2d8ff) for architectural cross-lighting. Add a dim AmbientLight (intensity:0.3). Interactions requiring parameter validation: 1) A 'Rotate Exhibit' slider (0-360 degrees) that rotates the entire pedestal group around the Y-axis, validate range. 2) A 'Spotlight Intensity' slider (0.0-5.0) that controls a SpotLight aimed at the helmet, validate numeric range. 3) A 'Spotlight Color' dropdown with options: Warm White (#ffe0b2), Cool White (#b2d8ff), Gallery Gold (#ffd700), Dramatic Red (#ff4444). 4) A 'Pedestal Spacing' slider (2.0-6.0) that adjusts the distance of flanking ShaderBalls from center, validate min/max. 5) A 'Camera Height' slider (1.0-8.0 meters) that adjusts the OrbitControls target Y and camera Y offset. 6) A 'Floor Reflectivity' slider (0.0-1.0) controlling the ground plane metalness. 7) A 'Capture View' button that logs current camera position, rotation, and all parameter values to a visible on-screen JSON panel. Create an HTML control panel fixed to the top-right with dark semi-transparent background. All sliders must display their current numeric value. Validate all inputs: clamp out-of-range values and reject non-numeric entries. Use GLTFLoader from three/addons. Set renderer with toneMapping=ACESFilmicToneMapping, toneMappingExposure=1.2, and antialiasing enabled. Scene background color #1a1a1a.

[State Exposure (required)]
Expose window.__3D_STATE__ updated every animation frame:
window.__3D_STATE__ = {
  exhibitRotationDegrees: number,         // current Y-axis rotation of the pedestal group in degrees (0-360)
  spotlightIntensity: number,         // intensity of the spotlight aimed at the helmet (0.0-5.0)
  helmetElevation: number,         // Y-position of the DamagedHelmet above the center pedestal
  pedestalCount: number,         // total number of ShaderBall pedestals loaded and positioned (should be 3)
  floorReflectivity: number,         // metalness value of the gallery floor material (0.0-1.0)
  warmLightIntensity: number,         // intensity of the warm directional light
  coolLightIntensity: number,         // intensity of the cool directional light
  cameraDistance: number,         // distance of the orbital camera from the scene center
  assetsLoaded: boolean,         // true when both DamagedHelmet.glb and all ShaderBall.glb instances are loaded
  controlsEnabled: boolean,         // true when OrbitControls are active and damping is enabled
  spotlightActive: boolean,         // true when the SpotLight targeting the helmet is added to the scene
  gridTextureApplied: boolean,         // true when the floor has a grid texture applied
}
